summary:
The term "high-angle" suggests significant anatomical changes related to the eye’s anterior chamber angle or its closure risk; thus, measuring parameters like FMP overcomes normal thresholds indicates this condition. Option C fits as \(FMP > 35^\circ\) implies notable alterations indicative of potential pathologies associated with increased intraocular pressure risks due to altered anatomy.
The selected answer highlights how specific angular measurements can signify clinically important deviations from normative values potentially linked to serious ocular health outcomes. Thus, choosing \(\boxed{C}\) accurately captures these essential aspects. Summary: A "high-angle" case involves significant anatomical changes at the ocular angle typically measured by indicators exceeding standard ranges, suggesting higher risk factors for conditions like angle-closure glaucoma. Selecting \(FMP > 35^\circ\) meets this criterion effectively.
